description_long: This object is a vintage, box-shaped cathode ray tube (CRT) television with a dark, matte exterior. The front features a large, convex glass screen with visible cracks and a thick, squared bezel framing the display. The casing is constructed from dark plastic or metal, with a utilitarian, blocky design typical of older television sets. The back panel is slightly protruded, housing a vented section and a central bulge likely for the CRT and internal electronics. There are no visible logos or branding, and the control buttons or dials are not clearly discernible from the images. The overall appearance suggests a heavy, robust appliance that would have been common in urban households or found discarded on city streets.
description: A cracked, vintage CRT television with a dark, boxy exterior and a thick bezel framing the damaged glass screen.
description_view_0: Side view showing the deep, rectangular body and the solid, unadorned casing.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the large, cracked glass screen and thick, dark bezel.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the depth and plain surface of the casing.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the protruding back panel, vented section, and central bulge for the CRT components.
attribute_bezel_design: thick, dark, squared bezel with a matte finish and no visible styling features
